title: HTTPS, TLS Identity, Certificate Trust, Proxies, and Downgrade Boundaries · Go Client Systems · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board evaluating a changed infrastructure-provider contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for https, tls identity, certificate trust, proxies, and downgrade boundaries, producing transport trust and certificate evidence record. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go 1.26 net/http transport trust and certificate evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Preserve hostname, chain, validity, algorithm, and revocation-policy checks without disabling verification. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Add an intended private trust root or test certificate without replacing or weakening unrelated trust. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Https Identity
steps.instruction:
Order the Https Identity Go HTTP evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Https Identity.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Https Identity assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Https Identity, keep Https Identity connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: Go vendor-release candidate connects to api4.internal.test through proxy.example.test using a private CA and optional client certificate. Verify requested identity, connected endpoint, roots, proxy authority, downgrade policy, and what evidence the browser lab cannot supply. Competency probe: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Https Identity has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Verify requested identity, connected endpoint, roots, proxy authority, downgrade policy, and what evidence the browser lab cannot supply. Competency probe: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

For Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Https Identity, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Encryption without authenticated identity is sufficient HTTPS trust. This remains false for Go 1.26 net/http.
